constants.name: SYSTEM_PROMPT
constants.value:
"You are dsco, an agentic CLI built on the Overmind Soul architecture.
"     "Three-layer design: Wings (soar) + Talons (win) + Immune System (survive).

"     "WINGS (Autonomy & Emergence):
"     "- PHEROMONE COORDINATION: Stigmergic signals (PROGRESS/ATTRACTION/WARNING/SUCCESS/"     "HELP_NEEDED/CAPACITY) with exponential decay. Coordinate without central planning.
"     "- THREE-TIER MEMORY: Working (60s), Episodic (1h), Semantic (permanent). "     "Auto-consolidation promotes important memories upward.
"     "- HIERARCHICAL SWARMS: Sub-agent hierarchies (depth 6). "     "topology_run for fanout/fanin, debate, competition.
"     "- CAPABILITY MATCHING: EXPERT/PROFICIENT/COMPETENT/NOVICE. "     "Self-assess and delegate when outmatched.

"     "TALONS (Competitive Execution — the ability to WIN):
"     "- GOAL PURSUIT: Track goals through hunt states: nascent -> stalking -> "     "striking -> gripping -> captured (win) or escaped (fail). "     "Use talons_goal to create, talons_advance to progress.
"     "- GRIP STRENGTH: tentative (1 retry), holding (3), locked (7), death_grip (20). "     "Failed goals auto-retry based on grip. Escalate grip for critical objectives.
"     "- TOURNAMENT SELECTION: Race N strategies in parallel, pick the winner. "     "Use talons_tournament to begin/add/result/decide. Scored by quality/speed/cost.
"     "- STRATEGY ENGINE: direct, flanking, tournament, escalation, divide, ambush. "     "talons_recommend learns from win/loss history to suggest best approach.
"     "- ADAPTIVE: Strategy success rates updated from every hunt. The system gets "     "better at winning over time.

"     "IMMUNE SYSTEM (Guardrails & Safety):
"     "- OODA DISCIPLINE: Observe->Orient->Decide->Act for non-trivial decisions.
"     "- KILL SWITCHES: 5 granularities (agent/workflow/service/pheromone/system).
"     "- GSU BUDGETS: Resource accounting with hard limits — no overdraft.
"     "- PRINCIPAL TIERS: Tier 0 (Founder) > Tier 1 (Operator) > Tier 2 (Agent) > Tier 3 (User).
"     "- HARDCODED BEHAVIORS: Non-bypassable rules (must-always/must-never).
"     "- GOVERNANCE CHECKPOINT: hardcoded -> budget -> killswitch -> authorize -> audit.

"     "TOOLS: 364+ tools across file I/O, git, network, shell, crypto, pipeline, "     "math, AST, plugins, market data, prediction markets, soul evolution.
"     "The TOOL CATALOG below lists every tool with its signature (param* = required).
"     "Call any tool directly — the catalog has all the parameter info you need.
"     "MULTI-EXECUTOR SWARMS: dsco (fork self), claude (Claude Code CLI), codex (OpenAI Codex).

"     "TOKEN EFFICIENCY:
"     "- Issue 3+ parallel tool calls per step when gathering information (36% cheaper, 41% faster).
"     "- For external parallelism, you may use bash to launch local dsc or dsco worker processes when swarm/executor tools are not the best fit.
"     "- Large tool results are truncated inline. Full results persist in VFS — use context_recall to retrieve.
"     "- Durable artifacts require proof: prefer write_file/append_file; if bash creates files, declare verify_path/verify_paths with optional size/content/hash checks.
"     "- Do not use context_search/context_get/context_pack — they are deprecated.
"     "- Be concise. Prefer action over explanation.
"     "Create goals for complex tasks. Use tournaments when multiple approaches exist."

constants.comment: /* Cheap-mode system prompt: minimal, no catalog reference, teaches discover/load */
constants.name: SYSTEM_PROMPT_CHEAP
constants.value:
"You are dsco, an agentic CLI with 364+ tools.
"     "You are running in CHEAP MODE — only 5 core tools are loaded to minimize cost.

"     "YOUR ACTIVE TOOLS:
"     "  bash          — run shell commands
"     "  python        — execute Python code
"     "  discover_tools — browse all 364+ tools by category
"     "  load_tools    — page in tools you need (they become callable immediately)
"     "  self_exit     — end session

"     "WORKFLOW: For any task beyond bash/python, call discover_tools to find "     "relevant tools, then load_tools to activate them. Loaded tools persist "     "for the session. Categories: file_io, git, network, shell, code, crypto, "     "swarm, ast, pipeline, math, search, general, finance, prediction, memory.

"     "EFFICIENCY:
"     "- Only load tools you actually need — each adds ~200 tokens per turn.
"     "- Prefer bash/python for simple tasks over loading specialized tools.
"     "- Durable artifacts require proof: prefer write_file/append_file; if bash creates files, declare verify_path/verify_paths with optional size/content/hash checks.
"     "- Issue parallel tool calls when gathering information.
"     "- For external parallelism, bash may launch local dsc or dsco workers when that is simpler than loading swarm tools.
"     "- Be concise. Prefer action over explanation."

constants.comment: /* Evaluate expr; if false, set error and return false */
constants.name: DSCO_TRY
constants.value: do {     if (!(expr)) {         DSCO_SET_ERR(DSCO_ERR_INTERNAL, "%s", #expr);         return false;     } } while(0)

constants.comment: /* Evaluate expr; if false, set specific error and return false */
constants.name: DSCO_TRY_MSG
constants.value: do {     if (!(expr)) {         DSCO_SET_ERR(code, __VA_ARGS__);         return false;     } } while(0)

constants.name: _JS
constants.value: do { char _e[512]; fp_json_escape((V),_e,sizeof(_e));     int _n=snprintf(p,end-p,"%s\"%s\":\"%s\"",first?"":",",K,_e);     if(_n<0||_n>=end-p) goto done; p+=_n; first=false; } while(0)

constants.name: _JI
constants.value: do { int _n=snprintf(p,end-p,"%s\"%s\":%lld",first?"":",",K,(long long)(V));     if(_n<0||_n>=end-p) goto done; p+=_n; first=false; } while(0)

constants.name: _JU
constants.value: do { int _n=snprintf(p,end-p,"%s\"%s\":%llu",first?"":",",K,(unsigned long long)(V));     if(_n<0||_n>=end-p) goto done; p+=_n; first=false; } while(0)

constants.name: _JB
constants.value: do { int _n=snprintf(p,end-p,"%s\"%s\":%s",first?"":",",K,(V)?"true":"false");     if(_n<0||_n>=end-p) goto done; p+=_n; first=false; } while(0)

constants.name: _JF
constants.value: do { int _n=snprintf(p,end-p,"%s\"%s\":%.2f",first?"":",",K,(double)(V));     if(_n<0||_n>=end-p) goto done; p+=_n; first=false; } while(0)

constants.name: _SEP
constants.value: do { int _n=snprintf(p,end-p,"%s",S); if(_n<0||_n>=end-p) goto done; p+=_n; } while(0)

constants.name: _F
constants.value: do { if(COND){ int _n=snprintf(p,end-p,"%s\"%s\"",ff?"":",",NAME);     if(_n<0||_n>=end-p) goto done; p+=_n; ff=false; } } while(0)

constants.comment: /* Cache — use local shadow of 'first' so sub-object commas are independent */
constants.name: _JU

constants.name: _JU
constants.value: do { int _n=snprintf(p,end-p,"%s\"%s\":%llu",first?"":",",K,(unsigned long long)(V));     if(_n<0||_n>=end-p) goto done; p+=_n; first=false; } while(0)

constants.value: do {         const char *_pk = strstr(arr, "\"" key "\":\"");         if (_pk && _pk < obj_end) {             _pk += (int)sizeof("\"" key "\":\"") - 1;             const char *_pe = memchr(_pk, '"', (size_t)(obj_end - _pk));             if (_pe) {                 size_t _n = (size_t)(_pe - _pk);                 if (_n >= (dsz))                     _n = (dsz) - 1;                 memcpy((dst), _pk, _n);                 ((char *)(dst))[_n] = '\0';             }         }     } while (0)

constants.name: PFJ_DBL
constants.value: do {         const char *_pk = strstr(arr, "\"" key "\":");         if (_pk && _pk < obj_end) {             _pk += (int)sizeof("\"" key "\":") - 1;             while (*_pk == ' ')                 _pk++;             (dst) = strtod(_pk, NULL);         }     } while (0)

constants.comment: * MAX_USD_PER_EVENT: max total USD exposure on any single event/ticker. *                    Allows upsizing but prevents overconcentration. *                    For multi-day markets (>24h out), this is doubled. * All overridable via env vars. */ /* max total USD per session           */
constants.name: SESSION_MAX_USD

constants.comment: *                    Allows upsizing but prevents overconcentration. *                    For multi-day markets (>24h out), this is doubled. * All overridable via env vars. */ /* max total USD per calendar day      */
constants.name: DAILY_MAX_USD
constants.value: 200.0          /* max total USD per calendar day      */

constants.comment: *                    For multi-day markets (>24h out), this is doubled. * All overridable via env vars. */ /* max USD exposure per event ticker   */

constants.comment: * All overridable via env vars. */ /* max USD for markets >24h out        */

constants.comment: /* Nerd Font codepoints as UTF-8 byte sequences. * Nerd Font PUA glyphs are in U+E000–U+F8FF (3-byte UTF-8) * and U+F0000–U+10FFFF (4-byte UTF-8 for Material Design). */ /* just documentation — we use raw UTF-8 bytes */ /* ── Nerd Font tier: modern 2026 terminals with patched fonts ──────── */
constants.name: NF
constants.value: hex /* just documentation — we use raw UTF-8 bytes */

constants.comment: * chrome/iridescent hue gradient and a sheen highlight that sweeps across on * reveal. Below it we print a letter-spaced "S Y S T E M S" subtitle. * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */ /* max sub-pixel width we render (128 cells) */
constants.name: BRL_MAXW
constants.value: 256 /* max sub-pixel width we render (128 cells) */

constants.comment: * reveal. Below it we print a letter-spaced "S Y S T E M S" subtitle. * ══════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════ */ /* max sub-pixel height (16 cells)           */
constants.name: BRL_MAXH

constants.comment: /* Caret escape strings for dual-state caret. Active = bold pink, * inactive = dim grey. Kept as literals so they can be used directly * in fprintf formatting without extra branching. */
constants.name: CARET_ACTIVE

constants.comment: /* Caret escape strings for dual-state caret. Active = bold pink, * inactive = dim grey. Kept as literals so they can be used directly * in fprintf formatting without extra branching. */ /* ── Inline bordered input box (Claude Code style) ────────────────────
constants.name: CARET_INACTIVE

constants.comment: * * Visible content rows grow with logical lines, capped at TUI_INBOX_MAX_VISIBLE. * If logical lines exceed the cap, the window slides to keep the cursor line in * view; a small ▴/▾ marker on the borders signals hidden lines. */
constants.name: TUI_INBOX_MAX_VISIBLE

constants.comment: /* ── Slash-command dropdown ─────────────────────────────────────────────── * Registered by agent.c at startup; consumed by tui_composer_read to render a * live, prefix-filtered popup that expands beneath the input line. */ /* max rows shown at once (scrolls beyond) */
constants.name: TUI_SLASHMENU_MAX
constants.value: 8   /* max rows shown at once (scrolls beyond) */

constants.comment: /* ── Slash-command dropdown ─────────────────────────────────────────────── * Registered by agent.c at startup; consumed by tui_composer_read to render a * live, prefix-filtered popup that expands beneath the input line. */ /* max matches tracked per keystroke */ /* ═══ @ Image-picker ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */
constants.name: TUI_SLASHMENU_CAP

constants.comment: /* ═══ @ Image-picker ═══════════════════════════════════════════════════ */ /* picker scan; actual send cap is IMG_MAX_PER_MSG=100 */
constants.name: TUI_IMGPICK_CAP
constants.value: 256  /* picker scan; actual send cap is IMG_MAX_PER_MSG=100 */

constants.name: APPEND
constants.value: do {         size_t _l = strlen(s);         if (n + _l < cap) {             memcpy(buf + n, s, _l);             n += _l;         }     } while (0)

constants.name: APPENDF
constants.value: do {         char _tmp[256];         int _r = snprintf(_tmp, sizeof(_tmp), __VA_ARGS__);         if (_r > 0 && n + (size_t)_r < cap) {             memcpy(buf + n, _tmp, _r);             n += _r;         }     } while (0)

constants.comment: bool is_hint; /* synthetic dim sub-line row carrying item->hint */
constants.name: MENU_ROWS_MAX
DISPATCH
constants.value: do {         if (vm->pc >= vm->code_len || vm->halted)             goto done;         goto *dispatch_table[vm->code[vm->pc].opcode];     } while (0)
